response.justification: The candidate is a highly skilled generalist software engineer with strong performance optimization and system architecture experience, but lacks the core hardware expertise required for this principal-level role. Critical gaps include no SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or deep neural network optimization background. While their AI-agent infrastructure exposure and low-latency optimization skills provide some foundation, they are fundamentally a software architect, not a hardware architect—and this role requires the latter at a senior level.
strengths: Extensive performance optimization experience across multiple domains (low-latency services, async I/O, caching, microservices at scale)
Deep technical foundation spanning 15+ years with systems thinking and architecture expertise across web, embedded, and infrastructure layers
Current exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and modern development practices with strong engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: No evidence of SoC/IP modeling, hardware design, or chip architecture experience—critical to the role's core responsibilities
Limited background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD optimization, or deep neural network training/inference acceleration
Career trajectory focused on application and service-layer software rather than hardware-software co-design at the infrastructure level

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP and GPU Architecture Expertise
AI Infrastructure Exposure in Current Role
